dict.string:
Hither bootstrap re-applier — defends /etc/synthetic.conf and /etc/auto_master against macOS update reverts.
Purpose: Re-apply the `Hither` entry to /etc/synthetic.conf and the `/Hither/{host} hither_{host} -nosuid` indirect-map entries to /etc/auto_master at every boot, and whenever those files are modified at runtime. This survives the macOS minor-update behavior of resetting /etc/auto_master to its shipping default (observed on 2026-05-15 when macOS 15.7.7 wiped both /Network/umbridge and /Network/hedwig entries that had been installed by the prior auto-smb-sync setup script).
Context: Runs as root at boot via RunAtLoad. Also runs whenever /etc/synthetic.conf or /etc/auto_master changes (WatchPaths). Performs ONLY local file edits + `apfs.util -t` + `automount -cv`. ZERO network calls — safe to run before Tailscale/DNS is up. ZERO mount probes (no stat/ls/cat under /Hither/{host}/) — autofs would attempt a root-context mount with no Keychain access, breaking the share.
Dependencies: requires /usr/local/sbin/hither-write-map to be installed (root-owned, mode 0755). Requires /etc/sudoers.d/xysat-hither-sync (granting infra-agent NOPASSWD on the wrapper). EnvironmentVariables.PATH is set explicitly because launchd-spawned processes inherit a minimal PATH (/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin); we extend it for the Hither CLI's own internal calls.
Operation: stdout → /var/log/hither/bootstrap.stdout.log; stderr → /var/log/hither/bootstrap.stderr.log. Use `sudo launchctl print system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to inspect state. Use `sudo launchctl kickstart system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to fire manually. Use `sudo launchctl bootout system/com.johnrandall.hither.bootstrap` to disable.
References: github.com/johntrandall/hither, admin-technical ADR-NNN (Hither lazy-mounter), field-report 2026-05-19 capturing the May-15 macOS-update auto_master revert that motivated this daemon.
